name: Turmeric
overview: Turmeric is a bright yellow-orange spice common in Indian, Southeast Asian, and Middle Eastern cooking. It has also been used as medicine in places like India for centuries to treat issues such as breathing problems.
traditional: A staple in Indian Ayurvedic medicine and Traditional Chinese Medicine for conditions like inflammation, digestive issues, and skin ailments.
active_compounds: Curcumin is the major active component, along with related substances called curcuminoids.
benefits: Potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Research supports its effectiveness in managing inflammatory disorders (e.g., arthritis), metabolic syndrome, anxiety, and muscle soreness.
safety_info: Generally considered safe. Its bioavailability is enhanced when taken with black pepper (bioperine).

name: Ginkgo Biloba
overview: Ginkgo (Ginkgo biloba) is one of the oldest living tree species and its leaves are among the most extensively studied herbs in use today.
traditional: Widely used in Chinese medicine for thousands of years, and one of the oldest homeopathic plants.
active_compounds: Contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are known for their potent antioxidant effects.
benefits: Known for boosting cognitive health, impacting anxiety, improving memory, and potentially helping with peripheral nerve damage.
safety_info: Ginkgo supplements can interact with blood thinners and may increase the risk of bleeding. Only leaf extract should be used.

name: Echinacea
overview: Echinacea is a group of flowering plants used as a popular herbal remedy. They're most commonly linked to helping your body fight off infections.
traditional: Used as medicine since Native American times, often as a booster treatment for the common cold and other infections.
active_compounds: Contains polysaccharides, alkamides, and caffeic acid derivatives.
benefits: May help boost the immune system, shorten the duration of colds, and potentially prevent their occurrence.
safety_info: Generally safe for short-term use. People allergic to plants in the daisy family may have an allergic reaction.

name: Chamomile
overview: Chamomile is an herb that comes from the daisy-like flowers of the Asteraceae plant family. It has been consumed for centuries as a natural remedy for several health conditions.
traditional: Noted as a medicinal tool in ancient Egyptian, Greek, and Roman practices, commonly prepared as a tea for calming properties and digestive aid.
active_compounds: Apigenin is a key bioactive compound that interacts with GABA receptors to promote relaxation.
benefits: Promoted as a remedy for anxiety, improving sleep quality, and alleviating gastrointestinal dysfunction.
safety_info: Considered safe by the FDA. May cause increased tiredness if taken with other medications.

name: Valerian Root
overview: Valerian is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. In the United States, it is widely used as a dietary supplement for sleep disorders and anxiety.
traditional: Used since ancient Greek and Roman times to treat insomnia, migraines, fatigue, and stomach cramps.
active_compounds: Contains valerenic acid, valepotriates, and volatile oils that are thought to interact with GABA receptors.
benefits: Promoted for improving sleep quality, reducing the time it takes to fall asleep, and managing mild anxiety.
safety_info: Generally safe for short-term use. Avoid prior to surgery or combining with other sedatives/alcohol.

name: St. John's Wort
overview: St. John's Wort is a flowering plant with a long history of use in traditional medicine for mental health conditions.
traditional: Historically used for anxiety, depression, and wound healing since ancient Greek times.
active_compounds: Hypericin and hyperforin are the primary active compounds responsible for its medicinal activity.
benefits: Extensively studied for mild-to-moderate depression; may also support menopausal symptoms and PMS.
safety_info: High potential for drug interactions. Can reduce the effectiveness of birth control and blood thinners. Consult a doctor before use.

name: Lavender
overview: Lavender is an aromatic evergreen shrub widely cultivated for its flowers and essential oils.
traditional: Used for over 2,500 years for its fragrance and therapeutic properties, from ancient mummification to Roman bathing rituals.
active_compounds: Linalool and linalyl acetate are the primary terpene alcohols responsible for its sedative effects.
benefits: Highly effective for reducing anxiety, improving sleep quality, and alleviating mild pain through aromatherapy and oral supplementation.
safety_info: Generally safe. Oral lavender oil can cause minor digestive upset. Topical use is safe for most, but skin irritation is possible.

name: Peppermint
overview: Peppermint is a natural hybrid cross between watermint and spearmint, widely recognized for its cooling sensation.
traditional: Used in ancient Egyptian, Greek, and Roman medicine for various digestive and respiratory ailments.
active_compounds: Menthol (up to 48%) and menthone are the primary active volatile oils.
benefits: Significant evidence supports its use for Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS), tension headaches, and soothing digestive discomfort.
safety_info: May worsen symptoms of GERD (Acid Reflux) by relaxing the lower esophageal sphincter. Use with caution during pregnancy.

name: Ashwagandha
overview: Ashwagandha, also known as Indian ginseng or winter cherry, is an evergreen shrub in the Solanaceae family. It is a cornerstone of Ayurvedic medicine, classified as a Rasayana herb for promoting health and longevity.
traditional: A key Rasayana herb in Ayurveda for over 3,000 years, traditionally used for rejuvenation, vitality, longevity, and to calm the nerves (Vataroga). The name 'somnifera' reflects its traditional use for sleep.
active_compounds: Steroidal lactones known as withanolides (withaferin A, withanolide A), alkaloids (withanine, somniferine), saponins, and flavonoids.
benefits: Proven to reduce stress, anxiety, and cortisol levels. Research also supports improvements in sleep quality, cognitive function, physical performance, and male fertility/sperm quality.
safety_info: Generally safe for up to 3 months. Avoid during pregnancy (potential abortifacient). May interact with thyroid (increases hormones), blood pressure, diabetes, and sedative medications. Use caution with liver disease or stomach ulcers. Stop 2 weeks before surgery.

name: Ginseng
overview: Ginseng refers to species in the Panax genus, primarily Asian (Panax ginseng) and American (Panax quinquefolius). The name Panax comes from the Greek word for 'all-healing'.
traditional: Highly prized in TCM (Traditional Chinese Medicine) as a 'Qi tonic' for energy and vitality. Used for over 2,000 years to improve physical function, immune health, and as an adaptogen.
active_compounds: Contains over 100 ginsenosides (steroidal saponins like Rb1, Rg1), gintonin, polysaccharides (ginsenans), and polyacetylenes.
benefits: Studies support benefits for cognitive function, mental clarity, fatigue reduction, immune modulation, and erectile dysfunction. May also help regulate blood sugar.
safety_info: Safe for short-term use (up to 6 months). Can interact with blood thinners (Warfarin), MAOIs, and diabetes medications. Avoid in hormone-sensitive conditions (estrogen-like effects), pregnancy, or autoimmune disorders. May cause insomnia or headaches.
